description: JOB DESCRIPTION: 勤務地：全国 ※採用ポジションの有無やご希望を踏まえ、配属先はご相談のうえ決定いたします。 About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. We’re always looking towards the future, anticipating changes in medical science and technology. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. We’re empowering smarter medical and economic decision making to help transform the way people manage their health at all stages of life. Every day, more than 10 million tests are run on Abbott’s diagnostics instruments, providing lab results for millions of people. 【業務内容】 臨床医療における診断・検査に不可欠な診断機器のメンテナンス業務となります。 アボットは臨床検査の分野では、癌やHIV・C型肝炎ウイルスなどの感染症やホルモン・甲状腺関連の診断薬を開発、提供するなど、幅広い事業領域で高い評価を得ています。 世界基準に照応したアボット ジャパンは世界から日本に、そして日本から世界へ、最先端の技術・製品・情報交流の重要な役割を担っています。 <具体的業務内容> ・機器の修理 ・定期点検 ・機器の設置 ・機器の改良、変更作業 ・保守契約の交渉、締結 ・保守部品の管理 <訪問先> 国公立病院・民間主要病院の検査室、日本赤十字社、臨床検査センターなど。 ※1日の訪問件数は1～2件程度。 ※訪問先に直行または直帰の場合も有り。 【応募条件】 ・機器メンテナンスまたはフィールドサービスの分野における3年以上の実務経験および知識 ・第一種普通自動車免許 ・PCスキル（Excel、Word、PowerPoint） ・医療機器業界での経験者優遇 Follow your career aspirations to Abbott for diverse opportunities with a company that can help you build your future and live your best life. Abbott is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to employee diversity. Connect with us at www.abbott.com , on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews and @AbbottGlobal. title: Senior TA Manager
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. As Senior Talent Acquisition Manager, you will play a critical role in setting the strategy and leading a team of internal recruiters and external partnerships to effectively attract, hire, and maintain a continuous pipeline of well-qualified talent. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Main purpose of position: This role will lead the talent acquisition strategy and hiring operations for Abbott Nutrition & Corporate Functions which includes talent acquisition support for all business and functional teams. Scope of role will ensure standardization of talent recruitment policies, procedures, practices, tools, & metrics, as well as deliver on the hiring requirements from business. Major areas of responsibility include developing and executing the recruiting strategies and solutions to meet/exceed the business staffing requirements, team management, stakeholder management. 2. Main responsibilities: Provide leadership and direction to a team of TA professionals and ensure Abbott attracts high caliber talent to meet business needs. Foster a continuous learning environment that champions a diverse and equitable culture. Support and lead project work by driving and executing against key expansion plans or functional projects and support local, regional PRM initiatives as appropriate. Establish the most effective recruitment processes and selection practices in. Execute with high quality hiring standards to meet diversity goals and provide exceptional candidate experiences . Own and manage the relationship with internal clients at senior manager, director, GM, occasionally up to DVP level . Deliver full cycle recruiting efforts and activities, from requirement definition to negotiation for leadership and most difficult to hire positions . May use the additional support of a sourcing specialist or a contingency search partner as required. Use cost-effective recruiting channels, implement direct sourcing initiatives, and create innovative campaigns to attract the best talent. MINIMUM BACKGROUND/ EXPERIENCE REQUIRED Work Experience : Minimum professional experience: 10 + years of recruiting experience with a min 3 years of in-house TA experience in a matrix environment. People management experience preferred. Functional experience: Full cycle recruitment experience in healthcare or adjacent industries and across main functions: commercial, scientific, and corporate function. Experience of supporting hiring needs at senior manager/director level across multiple functions, divisions or business units. Subject matter expert in developing marketing campaigns and direct sourcing strategies to engage, attract, and nurture passive candidates. Team leadership experience over a single contributor team or indirect leadership over resources assigned to a project and acquired in a matrix environment. Critical capabilities: Strategy development and execution Influence stakeholders and candidates Endurance and resilience Leadership Change management Data management, analysis and insights, story-telling Project management Language Skill: Advanced written and spoken English proficiency Vietnamese Computer Skill: Able to utilize MS computer programs such as notes, excel and PowerPoint to compile, build, analyses and consolidate data. MINIMUM EDUCATION REQUIRED Bachelor's Degree or equivalent combination of education and work experience The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. title: CRM Operations Manager I Head of Telesales
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: Job Description About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. We’re always looking towards the future, anticipating changes in medical science and technology. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. Our nutrition business develops science-based nutrition products for people of all ages, from helping babies and children grow, to keeping adult bodies strong and active. Millions of people around the world count on our leading brands – including Similac®, PediaSure®, Pedialyte®, Ensure®, Glucerna® and ZonePerfect® – to help get the nutrients they need to live their healthiest life. MAIN PURPOSE OF ROLE A critical role within the organization, reporting directly to the Head of CRM and working closely with Marketing, Commercial Excellence, IT, Procurement and external vendors to deliver campaign / program solutions that drive positive business outcomes. The role includes project and stakeholder management, prioritization, technology application and implementation, vendor management, performance management and tracking / reporting. Working Location: Abbott Office and Vendor Office (Tan Binh) Working Time: Mon - Sat Report to: CRM Director MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES Operational process design and implementation Target Operating Model. Working with the IT team and external vendors to source and implement the most appropriate technology solution for the business initiative Auditing/Identification and optimization of campaign strategies and other factors impacting program performance. Identification of trends, issues, gaps and performance improvement opportunities across all CRM programs on a continuous basis Provide strategic guidance and recommendations on increasing NU and sales for the brands Support strategic campaign planning activities including working with the Operations Manager for both Pediatrics & Adults and CRM Manager Analytics to identify key performance metrics to track, required resources and systems integrations required and development of end to end project plans Liaise with Brand teams, and other related parties on program setup, execution and operational reporting/analyses. Daily operations management duties include supervising campaign execution and resolving campaign operational issues Create and update internal CRM Operations procedure documents as needed Schedule campaign WIP meetings and circulate follow-up actions Manage and resolving campaign operational issue MINIMUM BACKGROUND/ EXPERIENCE REQUIRED Work Experience 5 – 8 years: • Experience in CRM including platform design and management, customer lifestyle segmentation, and omni-channel marketing. • Strong background in project and stakeholder management • Experience in developing customer acquisition, re-engagement and retention strategies • A Confident and articulate communicator capable of inspiring strong collaboration in an organization, equally confident in presenting to junior executives and senior leaders within the organization, tailoring the content and delivery accordingly • Excellent problem-solving skills along with the ability to understand and apply analytical judgment • Ability to break down complex problems into manageable goals and actions • Proven ability to navigate complex organization and build collaborative relationships across departmental lines • Knowledge of the latest industry technologies, able to review and provide the business with platform recommendations • Solid understanding of business planning, marketing testing and reporting methodology • Ability to multi-task and work under tight and/or shifting deadlines; Ability to prioritize tasks based on business impact and manage time appropriately within a fast-paced environment Education • Preferably in Marketing, Finance, Project Management, or General Business • Six Sigma and Salesforce certification will be a plus CORE Skills Required • Organization and planning kills • Analytical thinking • Reporting skills Behavioral / Leadership Skill (Competencies required for role) • Interpersonal skills • Problem solving skills • Time management Language Skill (any specific language required) • Good command of English Computer Skill (any specific Computer Skill required) • MS Office • SQL, Visio, MS Project, Six Sigma The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. Physician, Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist (Full-Time)
description: Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist MemorialCare Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach MemorialCare Medical Group Base Salary Range*: $300,000 to $310,000 *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position. Your Total Rewards would include a base salary plus performance-based incentives and other valuable benefits. Our compensation models utilize fair market value data specific to your specialty. Memorial Care Medical Group (MCMG) is looking for a Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist for our dedicated Pediatric Cardiothoracic Intensive Care Unit (CTICU) at Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach (MCWHLB). The new state of the art 8-bed CTICU and an additional 6-bed stepdown unit are part of our Heart Institute’s rapid growth to serve the needs of the southern California community. Candidates with strong clinical and teaching skills are encouraged to apply. The candidate should be board-certified in Pediatric Critical Care plus advanced fellowship training in Pediatric Cardiac Critical Care or a record of excellence in the clinical care of congenital cardiac patients. The successful candidate will work closely with the CTICU Medical Director Dr. Constantinos Chrysostomou and a multidisciplinary team of pediatric cardiac intensivists, cardiac surgeons, cardiologists, dedicated CTICU nurses and other specialists to further develop a state-of-the-art clinical program. Successful candidates should have (or be eligible for) an unrestricted California medical license. As part of the Memorial Care Medical Group, you will be given the tools you need to build the career you want, and the flexibility to adapt as your personal needs and professional interests change. We invite you to grow with us and help shape the future of this community. Robust compensation and benefits package are provided. Memorial Care Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach, is a private, not for profit children’s hospital first opened in 1970. It is a 324 bed children’s hospital that treats more than 8,000 children each year and has become a regional pediatric destination for more than 84,000 children. It has an active perinatal and maternal fetal medicine service with over 7000 deliveries annually to the adjacent birth center and one of the largest Neonatal Intensive Care units (NICU) in the area with over 90 beds. The hospital serves as the major referral center for the over 500,000 people of Long Beach, but reaches an additional 2 million people in the immediate vicinity. The hospital has an academic affiliation with the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) and the University of California Irvine (UCI) to which the successful candidate is eligible to apply for a faculty position. Financial & Benefits • Employed position with MemorialCare Medical Group • Competitive salary with incentive compensation • Eligible to be considered for Shareholder/Partner status with MCMG after two years of full-time employment • Full and comprehensive benefits for Physician and family • 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching • 529 College Savings Plan About Long Beach and Southern California Located on the coast of the Pacific Ocean south of Los Angeles and just west of Orange County, Long Beach is the sixth largest city in the State of California. Offering all the world class amenities of a large metropolitan city, coupled with its strong sense of community and pride, Long Beach is one of the most vibrant communities in the country. The city has been referred to as the "Most Diverse City" in the country by USA Today and one of the "Best Communities for Young People" two years in a row. With its ideal location in Southern California, year-round comfortable climate, healthy business environment and far ranging cultural pursuits, the city is alive with activity. Long Beach is home to an abundance of cultural and recreational options. Expansive beaches, three marinas, five golf courses, the Aquarium of the Pacific, the Queen Mary and the annual Grand Prix of Long Beach serve to draw 6.5 million visitors a year. Long Beach has easy access to all the amenities and attractions of Los Angeles, Orange, and San Bernardino counties, including professional sporting events, music festivals, gourmet dining, film and television studios, Disneyland, and snow skiing in the local mountains.

title: Physician, Aortic Surgery Fellow
description: Base Salary: $120,000 The MemorialCare Long Beach Heart and Vascular Institute is offering a non-ACGME accredited Advanced Aortic Surgery Training program with an anticipated start date of Summer 2026. Employment through MemorialCare Medical Group will end by July 31, 2027. We will consider candidates who are board-eligible or board-certified by the American Board of Thoracic Surgery. The fellowship will have exposure to advanced and complex open, hybrid, and endovascular aortic surgery, TAVR and minimal-invasive atrial fibrillation procedures. Basic and advanced catheter and wire skills training will be offered during the year. Furthermore, there is an opportunity to participate in minimal-invasive heart valve surgery such as mini right thoracotomy AVR or MVR operations. MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center is one of the largest hospitals on the West Coast, with affiliations with the University of California Irvine and the University of California Los Angeles. The Comprehensive Aortic program includes ten vascular and cardiovascular surgeons enhanced by access to the best medical technology, a state-of-the-art hybrid room, and a dedicated patient transfer program. The faculty includes pioneers in aortic and cardiovascular surgery including Dr. White, Dr. Khoynezhad, Dr. Abolhoda, Dr. Chiu, and Dr. Sakwa. This fellowship is a well-structured, mentored program that will involve a diversified experience. In addition to the operations and procedures, the fellow will participate in a research program that encompasses industry-sponsored multi-center trials, physician-sponsored investigational device exemption, and outcomes clinical studies. A publication in peer-reviewed journal and presentation at a national meeting is sponsored and a requirement of the fellowship. For the cardiovascular surgeon this program will include: Ascending aortic and hemi-arch surgery Aortic root replacement surgery including David/Yacoub Concomitant MAZE procedures (concomitant and left thoracotomy) Minimal-invasive AF procedures (total thoracoscopic, sub-xiphoid, hybrid) Descending and thoracoabdominal aortic replacement Aortic arch reconstructions including hybrid Advanced TEVAR Branched arch and thoracoabdominal aortic TEVAR Percutaneous ascending aortic TEVAR EVAR TAVR Minimally invasive mitral, aortic, and tricuspid valve surgery
